fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Massachusetts Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Massachusetts - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity massachusetts, how to transfer dietitian license to massachusetts, dietitian compact massachusetts, massachusetts dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Board of Registration of Dietitians and Nutritionists

quick_facts.ce_requirement: 30 continuing professional education units (CPEUs) every 2 years

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian/Nutritionist (LDN)
license_types.description: The unified credential to practice dietetics and nutrition in Massachusetts. Applicants may qualify through either the CDR (Registered Dietitian) or the BCNS (Certified Nutrition Specialist) examination pathway.

steps.title: Complete Required Education
steps.description: Complete a didactic program in dietetics and supervised practice, both accredited by the Accreditation Council for Education in Nutrition and Dietetics (ACEND).

steps.title: Pass the Registration Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the Registration Examination for Dietitians administered by the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR).

steps.title: Apply for Registration
steps.description: Submit a completed application and all required documentation, including transcripts and exam results, to the Massachusetts Board of Registration of Dietitians and Nutritionists.

documents.detail: Sealed by the registrar, verifying the highest degree earned. If your degree is not in a directly related field, include a completed 'Course Summary Form' with transcripts.
A copy of your current CDR registration card (for RDs) or an official sealed letter from BCNS confirming passage (for CNS applicants).
A 'Verification by Supervisor' form signed by your supervising dietitian, plus your professional resume.
Criminal Offender Record Information acknowledgment is required as part of the application.
If you hold or have held licenses in other jurisdictions, a certificate of standing must be obtained from each.

provisional_text: Massachusetts does not offer a provisional or temporary license for dietitians/nutritionists. Applicants must complete all requirements, including passing a Board-approved examination, before a license is issued.
reciprocity_text: The Massachusetts Board may grant reciprocal recognition to dietitians/nutritionists licensed in other jurisdictions, provided the standards of registration, certification, and licensure in the other state are 'reasonably equivalent' to Massachusetts requirements. Applicants must submit license verifications from all states where they hold or have held credentials.

faqs.question: How much does a Massachusetts dietitian/nutritionist license cost?
faqs.answer: The combined application and initial license fee is $196, payable to the Commonwealth of Massachusetts. The biennial renewal fee is also $196.

faqs.question: Which Massachusetts agency regulates dietitians?
faqs.answer: The Board of Registration of Dietitians and Nutritionists, which operates under the Massachusetts Department of Public Health (DPH), is the regulatory body.

faqs.question: What are the continuing education requirements for Massachusetts dietitians?
faqs.answer: Licensees must complete a minimum of 30 units (each unit = 50 minutes of organized learning) of continuing education in the field of dietetics and nutrition during each two-year renewal cycle. The Board may conduct random audits.

faqs.question: Does Massachusetts have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Massachusetts. To work in Massachusetts, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Massachusetts?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Massachusetts while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

reciprocity.notes: The Massachusetts Board may grant reciprocal recognition to dietitians/nutritionists licensed in other jurisdictions, provided the standards of registration, certification, and licensure in the other state are 'reasonably equivalent' to Massachusetts requirements. Applicants must submit license verifications from all states where they hold or have held credentials.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Massachusetts
hero.dek: Massachusetts Dietitian Registration requires completing an accredited dietetics program, passing the Registration Examination for Dietitians, and completing supervised practice hours.
